title: When the taxman comes for your cloud: The PIL that challenged India’s digital search powers
description: There is something quietly radical about the power to conduct a tax raid. It bypasses the ordinary rules of legal engagement. It requires no notice, no hearing, no opportunity to respond before officials are already at the door. Indian law has always defended this power on the logic of necessity. In tax raids, surprise is the point, because a warned taxpayer is a taxpayer who has already shredded the documents. Courts, including the Supreme Court, have historically accepted this logic and upheld
